source.section: AFCS (Automatic Flight Control System) > CPL (Coupled Steering) Mode > CPL TCN Sub-Mode Example

steps.action: Make sure your stick is centered, no force is applied on it and that you have an appropriate deadzone (see CONTROLS SETUP - WHAT YOU NEED MAPPED section).
steps.voiced: Confirm stick is centered with no input force and deadzone is configured.

steps.action: Select the desired TACAN station using the UFC. Verify that the TACAN is on.
steps.voiced: Select the desired TACAN station via the UFC — confirm TACAN is on.

steps.action: Activate the TCN navigation mode by pressing OSB next to TCN. TCN is boxed when selected.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to TCN to activate TCN navigation mode — confirm TCN boxes.

steps.action: If desired, click and hold (Left/Right mouse button) the CRS (Course Set) knob to set desired course (CSEL) to intercept the TACAN station from.
steps.voiced: Optionally set the CRS knob for desired intercept course.

steps.action: Press the AP button on the UFC to show the autopilot options.
steps.voiced: Press AP on the UFC to show autopilot options.

steps.action: Press the OSB adjacent “CPL” to activate coupled autopilot mode.
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to CPL to activate coupled autopilot mode.

steps.action: A colon will appear (“:CPL”) to indicate that it is active. “CPL TCN” will appear on both the HSI and HUD.
steps.voiced: Confirm :CPL is active and CPL TCN appears on HSI and HUD.

steps.action: The autopilot will follow the selected TACAN station.
steps.voiced: Confirm autopilot follows the selected TACAN station.

steps.action: You can disengage autopilot by pressing the Paddle Switch on the control stick.
steps.voiced: Press the Paddle Switch on the control stick to disengage autopilot.
